title: Supercooled kidneys have been transplanted into pigs in a “landmark achievement”
summary: When it comes to organ donation, time is everything. As soon as an organ has been carefully removed from a donor’s body, it starts to deteriorate. Surgeons have a matter of hours to get it into a recipient. Leave it too long and the organ will become unusable.    In most cases, organs will be kept on ice during that time, at around 4 °C (39 °F).…
